title: Quality gate: lint, tests, and MVP acceptance checklist
description: Verify the feature end-to-end against discovery MVP and architecture constraints: run the single documented verification command (or minimal documented set), confirm critical automated tests for rules and persistence pass, review layering and dependency rules from the architecture brief, and record structured pass/fail outcomes for the engineering manager report.
acceptance: `bun run lint` completes with exit code 0 on the delivery branch.
`bun run test` completes with exit code 0 on the delivery branch.

Governance increment checklist: `.gitignore` ignores `.data/`; `.env.example` documents server-only optional `TODO_SQLITE_PATH`; documented bootstrap creates gitignored `.env.local` with safe dev defaults and no secrets committed; README covers env copy flow and `bun run dev`; README covers default SQLite path (`<cwd>/.data/todos.sqlite` when env unset), lifecycle/reset, and OneDrive/sync caveats; no duplicate SQLite adapter or migration logic added in infra-only paths; no committed `.data/` or `.env.local`.
Documented verification command(s) from README execute successfully in a clean checkout scenario (dependencies installed per docs).
Automated tests cover critical domain rules and SQLite persistence behaviors agreed in prior tasks with no flaky reliance on developer-specific paths.
Manual or scripted checklist confirms todo CRUD with loading/error UX, safe server error messages, and migrations on a fresh SQLite file.

Static review confirms no forbidden imports in domain/application, no client exposure of DB paths/secrets, and styled-components hydration without critical console errors on Windows smoke path when available.
QA output uses structured pass/fail with issues list suitable for engineering-manager-agent handoff per qa-agent contract.
